title: hieracium triangulare subsp. euscepanum
intro: Hieracium triangulare subsp. euscepanum, also known as Hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, providing various health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Hawkweed is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions, or taken internally to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
